LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF ROBERTO VILLAREALE, QUANTRA PARTNERS SA APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER AND ORDER FOR ISSUANCE OF WRIT OF ATTACHMENT IS CONTINUED TO APRIL 25, 2017, AT 9:30 A.M., IN DEPT. 302, TO GIVE DEFENDANT AN OPPORTUNITY TO HIRE AN ATTORNEY AND FILE AN OPPOSITION. PLAINTIFF MAY AUGMENT THE APPLICATION FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER BY IDENTIFYING ADDITIONAL PROPERTIES THAT WILL BE SUBJECT TO THE ATTACHMENT, ON OR BEFORE MARCH 30, 2017. DEFENDANT MAY FILE AN OPPOSITION ON OR BEFORE APRIL 10, 2017, AND PLAINTIFF MAY FILE A REPLY ON OR BEFORE APRIL 18, 2017. THE PARTIES MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF ALL DOCUMENTS TO DEPT. 302, THE DAY AFTER THEY ARE FILED. DEFENDANT IS ORDERED NOT TO TRANSFER OR DISPOSE OF THE PROPERTY AT 27 COMMONWEALTH AVENUE, IN SAN FRANCISCO, BETWEEN NOW AND THE HEARING DATE. PLAINTIFF SHALL PREPARE THE PROPOSED ORDER. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR NO. 8189, EMAIL: CKAREN@SFTC.ORG; DEPT. 302.

LAW AND MOTION, 302, PLAINTIFFS ROBERTO VILLAREALE AND QUANTRA PARTNERS SA'S APPLICATION FOR A RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER AND A WRIT OF ATTACHMENT AGAINST DEFENDANT HERBERT KURLAN IS TAKEN OFF CALENDAR BECAUSE A REQUEST FOR ENTRY OF DEFAULT HAS ALREADY BEEN ENTERED IN THIS CASE. DEFENDANT CANNOT PARTICIPATE IN THIS PROCEEDING UNTIL THE DEFAULT IS LIFTED. THE COURT FINDS THAT IT COULD NOT GRANT A PROVISIONAL REMEDY WHICH IS IN THIS CASE A RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER. THE ORDER THAT WAS SIGNED EARLIER WAS NOT ENTERED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: CAROL KAREN CSR #8189 =302/HK

Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment
Off Calendar Apr-25-2017 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATY. DANIEL HARRIS OF THE CARLSON CALLADINE & PETERSON, TEL. NO. 1-415-391-3911 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
HERBERT KURLAN, TEL. NO. 1-415-850-5781, DEFENDANT APPEARING IN PRO PER.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS CALLED TWICE ON THE RECORD. THE COURT HAS ALREADY SIGNED AN ORDER WHEN IT WAS CALLED EARLIER. ON THE SECOND CALL OF THE MATTER, Plaintiffs Roberto Villareale and Quantra Partners SA's application for a right to attach order and a writ of attachment against defendant Herbert Kurlan is taken off calendar because a Request for Entry of Default has already been entered in this case. Defendant cannot participate in this proceeding until the default is lifted. The court finds that it could not grant a provisional remedy which is in this case a right to attach order. The order that was signed earlier was not entered. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 =302/hk

Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment
Off Calendar Mar-16-2017 Continued To Apr-25-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Daniel Harris, of Carlson, Calladine & Peterson, Tel. 415.391.3911, for plaintiffs Roberto Villareale & Quantra Partners SA;
Herbert Kurlan, in pro per, Tel. 415.850.5781, for defendants.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court modifies is tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF ROBERTO VILLAREALE, QUANTRA PARTNERS SA Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order and Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment is CONTINUED to April 25, 2017, at 9:30 a.m., in Dept. 302, to give defendant an opportunity to hire an attorney and file an opposition. Plaintiff may augment the application for right to attach order by identifying additional properties that will be subject to the attachment, on or before March 30, 2017. Defendant may file an opposition on or before April 10, 2017, and plaintiff may file a reply on or before April 18, 2017. The parties must deliver courtesy copies of all documents to Dept. 302, the day after they are filed. Defendant is ordered not to transfer or dispose of the property at 27 Commonwealth Avenue, in San Francisco, between now and the hearing date. Plaintiff shall prepare the proposed order. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.
